task: I need to send a short update to a few people about a work issue that’s still in progress, and I want to make sure I cover the key points without leaving anything important out. Can you help me draft a message that feels professional and easy to follow?

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — keep it short, clear, and focused on:

- what’s being checked
- why it’s still open
- when they’ll hear next

Here’s a simple version you can use:

> Hi সবাই — I’m still working through the issue and verifying the details before I give a final update. I want to make sure the information is accurate, so I’m checking the relevant records now. I’ll send the next update by [time/date]. Thanks for your patience.

If you want, I can also give you:
- a slightly warmer version
- a more firm/direct version
- a version for email vs. Slack/text

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, that’s basically the right shape, but the draft is a little too generic. I’d want it to say what stage it’s in and who’s handling it, without sounding like I’m hiding behind process.

Can you make it a little more specific and still short? Like: issue is being reviewed, I’m waiting on one more piece of info / confirming records, and I’ll update them by a certain time. Professional, plain, no fluff.

turns.agent_response:
Sure — here’s a tighter version that adds stage and ownership without sounding evasive:

> Hi all — this issue is still being reviewed. I’m waiting on one final piece of information and confirming the records before I send a full update. I’m handling it now and will follow up by [time/date]. Thanks for your patience.

If you want, I can also make it:
- a little warmer
- more direct
- shorter for Slack/text

user_simulator.text: Yeah, that works. Short, plain, and it doesn’t sound like I’m ducking it. I can use this as-is.
